title: Open navigation on explicit intent
statement: Navigation menus should open on explicit click rather than incidental hover; hover may reveal secondary local row controls.
rationale: Explicit activation avoids accidental navigation while preserving efficient disclosure of secondary actions.

prefer: Require a click or equivalent explicit activation to open navigation.
Keep hover effects local and non-navigational.
avoid: Opening a navigation surface because the pointer merely crossed it.

when: A menu or navigation surface changes place or reveals a substantial panel.
not_when: A platform-native pattern requires hover disclosure and is safely reversible.

evidence.summary: Required navigation dropdowns to open only after an explicit click and noted the intended behavior still failed.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Candidate pending independent recurrence or explicit owner approval.

positive_examples: Require a click or equivalent explicit activation to open navigation.
Keep hover effects local and non-navigational.
negative_examples: Opening a navigation surface because the pointer merely crossed it.

checks: Can pointer movement accidentally open or navigate?
